---
title: "Patient Resources"
toc: true
---

## Practical Tools and Resources

This page collects practical, actionable tools and resources for ME/CFS patients — things you can use today, separate from the scientific content of the paper.

### Hidden Disabilities Sunflower

The [Hidden Disabilities Sunflower](https://hiddendisabilitiesstore.com/) is a discreet, internationally recognised signal that the wearer has a non-visible disability and may need additional support, time, or understanding in public spaces.

ME/CFS is an invisible illness. The Sunflower lanyard lets you make it visible — on your own terms, without having to explain yourself every time.

**Where it's recognised:** airports, train stations, supermarkets, banks, hospitals, sports venues, universities, and many more — across 30+ countries worldwide.

**How to get one:** Lanyards are available free or at low cost directly from the Sunflower website. You do not need a formal diagnosis to wear one.

[Visit the Sunflower website →](https://hiddendisabilitiesstore.com/)

---

### Finding Recognised Venues

Use the [Sunflower location map](https://hdsunflower.com/find-the-sunflower) to find businesses and venues near you that recognise the Sunflower.

---

::: {.callout-tip}
## Also See

The [full Resources and Support appendix](z-appendices/appendix-d-resources/) contains a comprehensive guide to patient organisations, online communities, clinical trial registries, disability and legal resources, and more.
:::
